title: Luscious Kenyan Asparagus Broth
description: A spicy, glassy-jelly broth featuring fresh asparagus, coconut milk, and miso, cooked in a clay pot for a comforting Kenyan macrobionic dish.
instructions: Rinse the asparagus and cut the woody ends into 1-inch pieces.
Heat the clay pot over medium heat and add a teaspoon of vegetable oil, swirling to coat.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger, sautéing until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the sliced chili and tomato paste, cooking for two minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable stock, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Add the asparagus pieces, miso paste, and coconut milk, stirring to combine.
Simmer the broth for twenty minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the asparagus to soften.
Stir in the lemon juice and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Ladle the broth into bowls, topping each with a drizzle of coconut milk and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
Serve hot, and let the broth cool slightly to reveal its glassy-jelly consistency.
